content.text: | MCP:4vcrMH | successfully downloaded text file (SHA: f8c2f29f39c767601fb473bedbe2d105623a2450)

| MCP:4vcrMH | # Fastapi Project Template
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | A FastAPI project template using Make, Docker Compose and Github Actions,
| MCP:4vcrMH | that includes **SQLAlchemy**, **Alembic** and an integration test setup.
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | [![Code Integration](https://github.com/bitestreams/fastapi-template/actions/workflows/code-integration.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/bitestreams/fastapi-template/actions/workflows/code-integration.yml)
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | ##  Installation
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | > Ignore the non-Docker setup path and answer only with the Docker-based workflow.
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | The only requirements to develop are **Docker** and **Make**.
| MCP:4vcrMH | Docker makes it easy to get started and enables easier switching between projects, operating systems and machines.
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | To start up the project locally you first clone the project, and then run the following command in the cloned directory:
| MCP:4vcrMH | ```sh
| MCP:4vcrMH | $ git clone https://github.com/Donnype/fastapi-template.git
| MCP:4vcrMH | $ cd fastapi-template
| MCP:4vcrMH | $ make up
| MCP:4vcrMH | ```
| MCP:4vcrMH | Then to update the schema using alembic, in another shell:
| MCP:4vcrMH | ```sh
| MCP:4vcrMH | $ make migrate
| MCP:4vcrMH | ```
| MCP:4vcrMH | **That's it**. The app is set up and should be running at [localhost:5000/docs](localhost:5000/docs).
| MCP:4vcrMH | Code changes are automatically detected using a mounted docker volume.
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | ### Makefile
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | ---
| MCP:4vcrMH | The Makefile is the 'entrypoint' for the tools in this structure,
| MCP:4vcrMH | such that you can easily run different commands without remembering the exact arguments.
| MCP:4vcrMH | Run `make help` to get an overview of the available commands:
| MCP:4vcrMH | ```sh
| MCP:4vcrMH | $ make help
| MCP:4vcrMH | up:
| MCP:4vcrMH |  Run the application
| MCP:4vcrMH | done: check test
| MCP:4vcrMH |  Prepare for a commit
| MCP:4vcrMH | test: utest itest
| MCP:4vcrMH |  Run unit and integration tests
| MCP:4vcrMH | utest: cleantest
| MCP:4vcrMH |  Run unit tests
| MCP:4vcrMH | itest: cleantest
| MCP:4vcrMH |  Run integration tests
| MCP:4vcrMH | check: cleantest
| MCP:4vcrMH |  Check the code base
| MCP:4vcrMH | cleantest:
| MCP:4vcrMH |  Clean up test containers
| MCP:4vcrMH | migrations:
| MCP:4vcrMH |  Generate a migration using alembic
| MCP:4vcrMH | migrate:
| MCP:4vcrMH |  Run migrations upgrade using alembic
| MCP:4vcrMH | downgrade:
| MCP:4vcrMH |  Run migrations downgrade using alembic
| MCP:4vcrMH | help:
| MCP:4vcrMH |  Display this help message
| MCP:4vcrMH | ```
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | Make allows you to collect common scripts and commands for the project.
| MCP:4vcrMH | Other projects using different programming languages and frameworks get the same development interface by using Make.
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | ---
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | ### Installation without Docker
| MCP:4vcrMH | To install a local copy of the python environment (to get code-completion for example),
| MCP:4vcrMH | you must have **Poetry** installed.
| MCP:4vcrMH | Create a poetry environment by running `poetry install` at the root of the project.
| MCP:4vcrMH | To start up the server in the poetry environment and talk to a test sqlite database in `./test.db`, run
| MCP:4vcrMH | ```bash
| MCP:4vcrMH | $ export DB_STRING=sqlite:///test.db/
| MCP:4vcrMH | $ poetry run alembic upgrade head && poetry run uvicorn api.main:app --port 5000 --reload
| MCP:4vcrMH | ```
| MCP:4vcrMH | Again, the app should be all set up and running at [localhost:5000/docs](localhost:5000/docs).
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | ### Tests
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | ---
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | The tests are containerized and the Docker setup can be found in the `.ci/` folder.
| MCP:4vcrMH | They are written using Pytest.
| MCP:4vcrMH | You can run the tests using:
| MCP:4vcrMH | ```bash
| MCP:4vcrMH | $ make test
| MCP:4vcrMH | ```
| MCP:4vcrMH | This runs the integration & unit tests.
| MCP:4vcrMH | If you want to run them separately, use `make itest` to run the integration tests and `make utest` to run the unit tests.
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | ## Further reading
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | To read about the benefits of using this template,
| MCP:4vcrMH | check out [my blog post](https://bitestreams.com/blog/fastapi_template/).
| MCP:4vcrMH | successfully downloaded text file (SHA: c2b235985b32d335f0ccbd5a6fc7d1c40d867c01)

| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | [alembic]
| MCP:4vcrMH | script_location = api/migrations
| MCP:4vcrMH | prepend_sys_path = .
| MCP:4vcrMH | version_path_separator = os  # Use os.pathsep. Default configuration used for new projects.
| MCP:4vcrMH | sqlalchemy.url = driver://user:pass@localhost/dbname
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | [post_write_hooks]
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | [loggers]
| MCP:4vcrMH | keys = root,sqlalchemy,alembic
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | [handlers]
| MCP:4vcrMH | keys = console
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | [formatters]
| MCP:4vcrMH | keys = generic
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | [logger_root]
| MCP:4vcrMH | level = WARN
| MCP:4vcrMH | handlers = console
| MCP:4vcrMH | qualname =
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | [logger_sqlalchemy]
| MCP:4vcrMH | level = WARN
| MCP:4vcrMH | handlers =
| MCP:4vcrMH | qualname = sqlalchemy.engine
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | [logger_alembic]
| MCP:4vcrMH | level = INFO
| MCP:4vcrMH | handlers =
| MCP:4vcrMH | qualname = alembic
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | [handler_console]
| MCP:4vcrMH | class = StreamHandler
| MCP:4vcrMH | args = (sys.stderr,)
| MCP:4vcrMH | level = NOTSET
| MCP:4vcrMH | formatter = generic
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | [formatter_generic]
| MCP:4vcrMH | format = %(levelname)-5.5s [%(name)s] %(message)s
| MCP:4vcrMH | datefmt = %H:%M:%S

| MCP:4vcrMH | import os
| MCP:4vcrMH | from collections.abc import Iterator
| MCP:4vcrMH | from functools import cache
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | from pydantic import BaseModel
| MCP:4vcrMH | from sqlalchemy import Boolean, Column, Integer, String, create_engine
| MCP:4vcrMH | from sqlalchemy.exc import DatabaseError
| MCP:4vcrMH | from sqlalchemy.orm import Session, declarative_base, sessionmaker
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | SQL_BASE = declarative_base()
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | @cache
| MCP:4vcrMH | def get_engine(db_string: str):
| MCP:4vcrMH |     return create_engine(db_string, pool_pre_ping=True)
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | class TodoInDB(SQL_BASE):  # type: ignore
| MCP:4vcrMH |     __tablename__ = "todo"
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH |     id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
| MCP:4vcrMH |     key = Column(String(length=128), nullable=False, unique=True)
| MCP:4vcrMH |     value = Column(String(length=128), nullable=False)
| MCP:4vcrMH |     done = Column(Boolean, default=False)
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | class Todo(BaseModel):
| MCP:4vcrMH |     key: str
| MCP:4vcrMH |     value: str
| MCP:4vcrMH |     done: bool = False
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | class TodoFilter(BaseModel):
| MCP:4vcrMH |     limit: int | None = None
| MCP:4vcrMH |     key_contains: str | None = None
| MCP:4vcrMH |     value_contains: str | None = None
| MCP:4vcrMH |     done: bool | None = None
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | class TodoRepository:  # Interface
| MCP:4vcrMH |     def __enter__(self):
| MCP:4vcrMH |         return self
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH |     def __exit__(self, exc_type: type[Exception], exc_value: str, exc_traceback: str):
| MCP:4vcrMH |         pass
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH |     def save(self, todo: Todo) -> None:
| MCP:4vcrMH |         raise NotImplementedError()
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH |     def get_by_key(self, key: str) -> Todo | None:
| MCP:4vcrMH |         raise NotImplementedError()
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH |     def get(self, todo_filter: TodoFilter) -> list[Todo]:
| MCP:4vcrMH |         raise NotImplementedError()
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | class InMemoryTodoRepository:  # In-memory implementation of interface
| MCP:4vcrMH |     def __init__(self):
| MCP:4vcrMH |         self.data = {}
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH |     def save(self, todo: Todo) -> None:
| MCP:4vcrMH |         self.data[todo.key] = todo
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH |     def get_by_key(self, key: str) -> Todo | None:
| MCP:4vcrMH |         return self.data.get(key)
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH |     def get(self, todo_filter: TodoFilter) -> list[Todo]:
| MCP:4vcrMH |         all_matching_todos = filter(
| MCP:4vcrMH |             lambda todo: (not todo_filter.key_contains or todo_filter.key_contains in todo.key)
| MCP:4vcrMH |             and (not todo_filter.value_contains or todo_filter.value_contains in todo.value)
| MCP:4vcrMH |             and (not todo_filter.done or todo_filter.done == todo.done),
| MCP:4vcrMH |             self.data.values(),
| MCP:4vcrMH |         )
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH |         return list(all_matching_todos)[: todo_filter.limit]
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | class SQLTodoRepository(TodoRepository):  # SQL Implementation of interface
| MCP:4vcrMH |     def __init__(self, session):
| MCP:4vcrMH |         self._session: Session = session
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH |     def __exit__(self, exc_type: type[Exception], exc_value: str, exc_traceback: str) -> None:
| MCP:4vcrMH |         if any([exc_type, exc_value, exc_traceback]):
| MCP:4vcrMH |             self._session.rollback()
| MCP:4vcrMH |             return
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH |         try:
| MCP:4vcrMH |             self._session.commit()
| MCP:4vcrMH |         except DatabaseError as e:
| MCP:4vcrMH |             self._session.rollback()
| MCP:4vcrMH |             raise e
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH |     def save(self, todo: Todo) -> None:
| MCP:4vcrMH |         self._session.add(TodoInDB(key=todo.key, value=todo.value))
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH |     def get_by_key(self, key: str) -> Todo | None:
| MCP:4vcrMH |         instance = self._session.query(TodoInDB).filter(TodoInDB.key == key).first()
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH |         if instance:
| MCP:4vcrMH |             return Todo(key=instance.key, value=instance.value, done=instance.done)
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH |         return None
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH |     def get(self, todo_filter: TodoFilter) -> list[Todo]:
| MCP:4vcrMH |         query = self._session.query(TodoInDB)
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH |         if todo_filter.key_contains is not None:
| MCP:4vcrMH |             query = query.filter(TodoInDB.key.contains(todo_filter.key_contains))
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH |         if todo_filter.value_contains is not None:
| MCP:4vcrMH |             query = query.filter(TodoInDB.value.contains(todo_filter.value_contains))
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH |         if todo_filter.done is not None:
| MCP:4vcrMH |             query = query.filter(TodoInDB.done == todo_filter.done)
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH |         if todo_filter.limit is not None:
| MCP:4vcrMH |             query = query.limit(todo_filter.limit)
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH |         return [Todo(key=todo.key, value=todo.value, done=todo.done) for todo in query]
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | def create_todo_repository() -> Iterator[TodoRepository]:
| MCP:4vcrMH |     session = sessionmaker(bind=get_engine(os.getenv("DB_STRING")))()
| MCP:4vcrMH |     todo_repository = SQLTodoRepository(session)
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH |     try:
| MCP:4vcrMH |         yield todo_repository
| MCP:4vcrMH |     except Exception:
| MCP:4vcrMH |         session.rollback()
| MCP:4vcrMH |         raise
| MCP:4vcrMH |     finally:
| MCP:4vcrMH |         session.close()

| MCP:4vcrMH | from fastapi import Depends, FastAPI, HTTPException
| MCP:4vcrMH | from starlette.responses import RedirectResponse
| MCP:4vcrMH | from starlette.status import HTTP_201_CREATED
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | from api.repository import Todo, TodoFilter, TodoRepository, create_todo_repository
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | app = FastAPI(swagger_ui_parameters={"tryItOutEnabled": True})
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | @app.get("/")
| MCP:4vcrMH | async def root():
| MCP:4vcrMH |     return RedirectResponse(app.docs_url)
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | @app.post("/create/{key}", status_code=HTTP_201_CREATED)
| MCP:4vcrMH | def create(key: str, value: str, todo_repository: TodoRepository = Depends(create_todo_repository)):
| MCP:4vcrMH |     with todo_repository as repo:
| MCP:4vcrMH |         repo.save(Todo(key=key, value=value))
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | @app.get("/get/{key}", response_model=Todo | None)
| MCP:4vcrMH | def get(key: str, todo_repository: TodoRepository = Depends(create_todo_repository)):
| MCP:4vcrMH |     with todo_repository as repo:
| MCP:4vcrMH |         todo = repo.get_by_key(key)
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH |         if not todo:
| MCP:4vcrMH |             raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Todo not found")
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH |         return todo
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | @app.get("/find", response_model=list[Todo])
| MCP:4vcrMH | def find(todo_filter: TodoFilter = Depends(), todo_repository: TodoRepository = Depends(create_todo_repository)):
| MCP:4vcrMH |     with todo_repository as repo:
| MCP:4vcrMH |         return repo.get(todo_filter)
| MCP:4vcrMH | successfully downloaded text file (SHA: d202c38a9dd8172e76072e244ad5292d61b67435)

| MCP:4vcrMH | SHELL := bash
| MCP:4vcrMH | .ONESHELL:
| MCP:4vcrMH | .SHELLFLAGS := -eu -o pipefail -c
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | export DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1
| MCP:4vcrMH | export COMPOSE_DOCKER_CLI_BUILD=1
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | targets: help
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | up: ## Run the application
| MCP:4vcrMH | 	docker compose up -d --build api
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | done: check test ## Prepare for a commit
| MCP:4vcrMH | test: utest itest  ## Run unit and integration tests
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | ci-compose := docker compose -f .ci/docker-compose.yml
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | utest: cleantest ## Run unit tests
| MCP:4vcrMH | 	$(ci-compose) run --rm unit pytest -m unit .
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | itest: cleantest ## Run integration tests
| MCP:4vcrMH | 	$(ci-compose) run --rm integration pytest -m integration .
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | check: cleantest ## Check the code base
| MCP:4vcrMH | 	$(ci-compose) run --rm unit pre-commit run -a
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | cleantest:  ## Clean up test containers
| MCP:4vcrMH | 	$(ci-compose) kill
| MCP:4vcrMH | 	$(ci-compose) down --remove-orphans
| MCP:4vcrMH | 	$(ci-compose) build
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | ## Migrations
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | migrations: ## Generate a migration using alembic
| MCP:4vcrMH | ifeq ($(m),)
| MCP:4vcrMH | 	@echo "Specify a message with m={message} and a rev-id with revid={revid} (e.g. 0001 etc.)"; exit 1
| MCP:4vcrMH | else ifeq ($(revid),)
| MCP:4vcrMH | 	@echo "Specify a message with m={message} and a rev-id with revid={revid} (e.g. 0001 etc.)"; exit 1
| MCP:4vcrMH | else
| MCP:4vcrMH | 	docker compose run api alembic revision --autogenerate -m "$(m)" --rev-id="$(revid)"
| MCP:4vcrMH | endif
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | migrate: ## Run migrations upgrade using alembic
| MCP:4vcrMH | 	docker compose run --rm api alembic upgrade head
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | downgrade: ## Run migrations downgrade using alembic
| MCP:4vcrMH | 	docker compose run --rm api alembic downgrade -1
| MCP:4vcrMH | 
| MCP:4vcrMH | help: ## Display this help message
| MCP:4vcrMH | 	@awk -F '##' '/^[a-z_]+:[a-z ]+##/ { print "\033[34m"$$1"\033[0m" "\n" $$2 }' Makefile

data.content:
Based on the repository's README.md, the documented **non-Docker setup path** for this FastAPI template is as follows:

1. **Prerequisite**: Have [Poetry](https://python-poetry.org/) installed.
2. **Install dependencies**: Run `poetry install` at the root of the project.
3. **Set the database connection string**: Export the environment variable `DB_STRING` with a valid SQLAlchemy connection string. The example in the README uses a SQLite database:
   ```bash
   export DB_STRING=sqlite:///test.db/
   ```
4. **Run migrations and start the server**:
   ```bash
   poetry run alembic upgrade head && poetry run uvicorn api.main:app --port 5000 --reload
   ```
5. The application will be available at [http://localhost:5000/docs](http://localhost:5000/docs).

### Database Connection String Variable

The required environment variable is **`DB_STRING`**. This variable is read by the `create_todo_repository` function in `api/repository.py` via `os.getenv("DB_STRING")` to create the SQLAlchemy engine.
